channel.title: FMaInewsletter
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from FMaInewsletter, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: FCA Finfluencer Rules: Key Compliance Steps for Financial Firms
item.description: The FCA's crackdown on finfluencers requires firms to treat social media financial promotions as a formal compliance function, with new oversight obligations.

item.title: Big Four Accounting Firms Reduce Benefits and Hiring as AI Expands
item.description: Major accounting firms are cutting employee benefits and scaling back hiring as artificial intelligence takes on more tasks traditionally performed by human workers.

item.title: Cohere's European Expansion Signals Growth of AI Powers Outside US and China
item.description: Cohere's partnership with German-backed Aleph Alpha points to a growing bloc of AI companies positioning themselves between dominant US and Chinese players.

item.title: Copy Trading in the UK: Weighing the Potential and the Risks in 2026
item.description: Copy trading lets investors mirror experienced traders automatically, but the strategy carries notable risks that prospective users should carefully evaluate before committing funds.

item.title: Amazon Removes Controversial 1973 Novel; Foreword Author Responds
item.description: Amazon has delisted Jean Raspail's 1973 novel, prompting a response from the author of its foreword, who questions the retailer's content removal decisions.
